title: Best Buy Tops Estimates While Keeping ‘Shrinkage' At Bay
text: While Best Buy is feeling a pullback in consumer spending they have not experienced the high levels of theft that has devastated other retailers' bottom lines.

title: PCE Inflation In-Line With Expectations
text: Normally, the week prior to the long Labor Day weekend is very quiet for stock market news — many investors are just now re-directing their yachts back toward their slip — but this week is a bit of an anomaly: economic data abounds, along with major quarterly earnings reports from companies like Salesforce ( CRM ) and Best Buy ( BBY ), both which outperformed expectations. As such, those of us who still have our ear to the ground may be better prepared to situate ourselves post-Labor Day.

title: Is It Time to Buy Best Buy Stock?
text: Weak demand for PCs, smartphones, and appliances is wreaking havoc on Best Buy's sales. The retailer remains profitable, and its margins are holding up well despite the tough environment.

title: Best Buy Announces Regular Quarterly Cash Dividend
text: MINNEAPOLIS--(BUSINESS WIRE)--The Board of Directors of Best Buy Co., Inc. (NYSE:BBY) has authorized the payment of a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.92 per common share. The quarterly dividend is payable on October 10, 2023, to shareholders of record as of the close of business on September 19, 2023. The company had 217,928,957 shares of common stock issued and outstanding as of July 29, 2023.

title: Best Buy Tops Quarterly Expectations but Lowers Outlook on Falling Sales
text: Best Buy (BBY) on Tuesday reported better-than-expected earnings for its latest fiscal quarter, but acknowledged a challenging sales backdrop as consumers bought fewer electronics and appliances.

title: Best Buy (BBY) Q2 Earnings Beat, Comparable Sales Dip 6.2% Y/Y
text: Best Buy (BBY) reports better-than-expected second-quarter fiscal 2024 earnings. However, its sales decline from the year-ago quarter due to weak enterprise comparable sales.

title: Best Buy declares the 'low point' in tech sales after years of declines
text: Best Buy's CEO said we're in "the low point in tech demand after two years of sales declines." Shoppers are spending more on experiences and preparing for the return of student-loan repayments.

title: Best Buy triples down on call for bottom in tech demand
text: Best Buy Co. Inc. Chief Executive Corie Barry is making the call for a bottom in tech demand on the consumer-electronics retailer's post-earnings conference call with analysts, according to an AlphaSense transcript.

title: Best Buy expects tech demand to hit a low this year
text: Best Buy expects declining tech sales to hit a low this year before stabilizing, in another sign of consumer caution around discretionary spending and retail purchases.

title: Best Buy Stock Brushes Off Slashed Revenue Outlook
text: Best Buy Co Inc   (NYSE:BBY ) is up 5.1% to trade at $77.80 this morning, after the retailer topped analysts' second-quarter earnings and revenue expectations.

title: Early movers: Best Buy, Axcella, Smucker, BYD, NIO, VinFast, and more
text: Best Buy ‘beats' Best Buy Co Inc (NYSE:BBY) stock started Tuesday on the front, up 5.3% at $77.99, after it reported second-quarter results that were ahead of market expectations, whilst also reflecting a normalization of spending patterns and the inflationary pinch on sales. Revenue for the quarter was $9.58 billion, beating analyst forecasts of $9.52 billion, but shy of last year's comparative of $10.33 billion.

title: Best Buy (BBY) Tops Q2 Earnings and Revenue Estimates
text: Best Buy (BBY) came out with quarterly earnings of $1.22 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.06 per share. This compares to earnings of $1.54 per share a year ago.

title: Best Buy stock boosted by ‘smaller than feared' drop in revenue
text: Best Buy Co Inc (NYSE:BBY) stock started Tuesday on the front after it reported second quarter results that were ahead of market expectations, whilst also reflecting a normalization of spending patterns and the inflationary pinch on sales. Revenue for the quarter was marked at $9.58 billion, beating analyst forecasts for $9.52 billion, but, shy of last year's comparative of $10.33 billion.

title: Best Buy Beats Second-Quarter Views, Sees Business Stabilizing Next Year

title: Best Buy scales back sales outlook as results top expectations
text: While Best Buy trimmed its sales guidance for the year, it raised the bottom end of its profit outlook.

title: Best Buy stock gains after earnings beat but mixed outlook, sees tech sales bottoming this year
text: Shares of Best Buy Co. Inc. BBY, +1.93% got a 0.5% boost in premarket trading Tuesday, after the consumer electronics retailer reported fiscal second-quarter results that beat expectations, while providing a mixed full-year outlook. Net income for the quarter to July 29 fell to $274 million, or $1.25 a share, from $306 million, or $1.35 a share, in the year-ago period.

title: Markets Up Second-Straight Day; Lots of Data On Deck

title: Markets Await Jobs Data
text: Pre-market futures are inching back into the green to start a new week — one that will being the end of August 2023, which is headed for the worst performance on the blue-chip Dow since May (-3.4%) and the worst for the S&P 500 (-4%) since December of last year. The Nasdaq has performed even worse: -5.3% month to date, and the more volatile small-cap Russell 2000 really would welcome the end of this trading month: -7.4%.

title: These 20 dividend stocks have been the best income growers in the S&P 500

title: Best Buy Is Taking Steps, But Has A Short Stride
text: Best Buy has successfully adapted to the digital shift in consumer behavior, with digital sales accounting for a significant portion of its revenue. The introduction of paid membership programs is expected to contribute positively to Best Buy's financial performance in the coming years. While Best Buy has made improvements in various areas, its financials, valuation, and growth prospects do not justify a buy recommendation.

title: My Top 10 High Yield Dividend Stocks For August 2023

title: Best Buy Appoints Dave Kimbell to Board of Directors
text: MINNEAPOLIS--(BUSINESS WIRE)---- $BBY #BBYBlackFriday--Best Buy Co., Inc. has appointed David C. Kimbell, a leading specialty retail executive, to its Board of Directors, effective immediately. Kimbell is the Chief Executive Officer of Ulta Beauty, the largest specialty beauty retailer in the U.S., and has held the role since June 2021. He also sits on its Board of Directors. Since joining Ulta Beauty in 2014, he has served in leadership roles including President and Chief Merchandising and Marketing Officer. Kimbell i.
